differentials.raw: Cystic neuroendocrine tumor (c)  solid pancreatic neoplasms with cystic degeneration

context: Frequency:   < 10% of all pancreatic neoplasms (a)  common cystic pancreatic neoplasms (5-15%) RCC, melanoma, lung tumors, breast carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, ovarian carcinoma Features of malignancy: √  > 10 mm main pancreatic duct dilatation √  > 10 mm large mural node √  > 3 cm large cyst √  > 2.5 SUV positivity on FDG √  irregular / septate features √  calcification Imaging performance for malignant cystic lesion: FDG-PET:  56% sensitive, 83% specific CT:  81% sensitive, 100% specific PET/CT:  94%sensitive, 100% specific MRCP can image the pancreatic duct after Whipple procedure, d
